meta.question: Was Dovie Byrd a daughter of John Archie Asner Byrd + Martha Alice Bedford?
meta.summary: Hypothesis is internally consistent and geographically plausible, but no primary source confirms Dovie as a child of John A Byrd. The question cannot be upgraded beyond POSSIBLE with freely accessible open-web records.

confidence_scale.CONFIRMED: Primary source documentary record cited (burial index, vital record, newspaper)
confidence_scale.PROBABLE: 2+ corroborating secondary sources, no single record states the full claim
confidence_scale.POSSIBLE: Plausible from single source or logical inference
confidence_scale.NOT_FOUND: Searched, not found in this pass

confirmed_records.burial_place: Sharp Cemetery, Sharp, Milam County, Texas
notes: Exact date match with KcaMarco GEDCOM parameters (born 1868, died 1928)
PeopleLegacy record does not confirm Birmingham birthplace or middle name Archie/Asner
Only 'John A Byrd' in the burial index

confirmed_records.burial_place: Restwood Memorial Park, Clute, Brazoria County, Texas
confirmed_records.burial_address: 1038 West Lake Jackson Rd, Clute, TX 77566
confirmed_records.confidence: CONFIRMED for burial; UNVERIFIED for parentage
notes: Exact date match with KcaMarco GEDCOM (born 1902, died 1989)
PeopleLegacy does NOT list parents
Parentage comes only from KcaMarco GEDCOM — not independently confirmed

confirmed_records.burial_place: Sharp Cemetery, Sharp, Milam County, Texas
notes: Possible son of John A Byrd — same cemetery, plausible 20-year age gap

confirmed_records.burial_place: Sharp Cemetery, Sharp, Milam County, Texas
notes: Possible son of John A Byrd — same cemetery, plausible 24-year age gap

confirmed_records.burial_place: Sharp Cemetery, Sharp, Milam County, Texas
notes: Age (b. 1829) suggests John A Byrd's mother or grandmother — approximately 39 years older than John

confirmed_records.burial_place: Restwood Memorial Park, Clute, Brazoria County, Texas
confirmed_records.burial_address: 1038 West Lake Jackson Rd, Clute, TX 77566
notes: Buried at same cemetery and address as Otha Lee Byrd Sr.
Age (b. 1877) is consistent with being Otha's mother if she married John A Byrd (b. 1868)
INCONSISTENCY: KcaMarco GEDCOM names Otha's mother as 'Martha Alice Bedford', not 'Lillie'
This inconsistency is unresolved — Lillie could be a sister, aunt, or the GEDCOM may have an error

not_found.search: Dovie Byrd burial record in Texas
notes: PeopleLegacy Texas filter for Dovie Byrd returns only 'Dovie Phillips Byrd' (1916-2002, Jenkins TX) — she married INTO the Byrd family, not a Byrd by birth
No burial record for a Dovie Byrd born 1900-1915 in Texas was found in open records

County-level marriage index requires Ancestry/FamilySearch login for pre-1966 records

Find a Grave blocked by Cloudflare protection

Texas birth index not accessible via open web in this research pass

Q1_Dovie_born_to_John_Archie_Byrd.explanation: No primary source confirms Dovie as a child of John A Byrd. The burial records for John A Byrd (Sharp Cemetery) do not list children.

candidates.name: William Luther Byrd
candidates.basis: Same cemetery as father, 20-year age gap

candidates.name: James Lee Byrd
candidates.basis: Same cemetery as father, 24-year age gap

Q2_full_child_list_of_John_Archie_Byrd.explanation: Census records (1910 Milam Co) would be definitive. Open web sources did not yield a complete child list.

Q4_Dovie_birth_year_birthplace.basis: Backward calculation from Dale Sr. birth ~1934-1936; John A Byrd (1868) would be 32-50 years old — plausible for a later child

geographic_analysis.summary: Geographic migration pattern is consistent with the hypothesis
geographic_analysis.pattern: Milam County TX (Sharp) -> Gulf Coast TX (Clute/Beaumont area), 1920s-1940s
geographic_analysis.note: Oil and chemical industry corridor from Spindletop (1901) through Brazoria Co and Jefferson Co drew migration from Central Texas. If Dovie married a Spence and moved to Beaumont (Jefferson Co) while Otha moved to Clute (Brazoria Co), both followed the same documented regional migration pattern.

inconsistency_flag.detail: KcaMarco GEDCOM says Otha's mother was 'Martha Alice Bedford'. PeopleLegacy shows 'Lillie Byrd' (1877-1948) buried at same cemetery as Otha in Clute TX. These do not match.
possible_explanations: GEDCOM may have an error — Lillie could be the actual mother
Lillie could be Otha's sister, aunt, or unrelated person buried nearby
The Clute Byrd family may not be connected to the Sharp Cemetery Byrd family at all
John A Byrd may have remarried — first wife Lillie, then Martha Alice Bedford (or vice versa)

next_steps.expected_yield: If Dovie married by 1930, she would appear with husband and children including Dale Sr.
